gloss.en: a time of great danger or difficulty

words.example: The country is facing an economic crisis.

gloss.en: a standard used to judge or decide something

words.example: The main criterion is value for money.

gloss.en: a person who judges books, films, or music

words.example: She works as a film critic for a newspaper.

gloss.en: saying what you think is bad about something

words.example: You should just ignore any critical comments.

gloss.en: in a way that shows disapproval

words.example: She spoke critically of her father.

gloss.en: the act of saying what is bad about something

words.example: The plan received a lot of criticism.

gloss.en: to say that someone or something is bad

words.example: All you ever do is criticize!

gloss.en: a large reptile with a long mouth and teeth

words.example: A crocodile lay still by the river.

gloss.en: a plant grown in large amounts for food

words.example: Sugar is an important crop on the island.

gloss.en: in an angry or annoyed way

words.example: He looked at me crossly and said nothing.

gloss.en: extremely important; decisive

words.example: The next few weeks are going to be crucial.

gloss.en: in a way that causes pain or suffering

words.example: The animals were treated cruelly.

gloss.en: behaviour that causes pain to others

words.example: He was shocked by such cruelty to animals.

gloss.en: a holiday trip by sea to many places

words.example: I'd love to go on a round-the-world cruise.

gloss.en: a loud sound showing a strong feeling

words.example: She gave a cry of pain.

gloss.en: to form crystals; to become clear and definite

words.example: His ideas began to crystallise into a plan.

gloss.en: to form crystals; to become clear and definite

words.example: His ideas began to crystallize into a plan.

gloss.en: a solid shape with six equal square sides

words.example: She dropped a cube of ice into her drink.

gloss.en: an art style using simple shapes and angles

words.example: Picasso was a leading figure in cubism.

gloss.en: a signal for someone to do something

words.example: Jon's arrival was a cue for more champagne.

gloss.en: the end of a sleeve around the wrist

words.example: He rolled up the cuff of his shirt.

gloss.en: the highest point or final result of something

words.example: The award was the culmination of her career.

gloss.en: in a way relating to culture

words.example: The two regions are culturally very different.

gloss.en: a medicine or treatment that ends an illness

words.example: Scientists are searching for a cure for cancer.

gloss.en: wanting to know about something

words.example: He is such a curious boy, always asking questions.

gloss.en: a curved or coiled piece of hair

words.example: A loose curl fell across her face.

gloss.en: happening now; of the present time

words.example: The necklace would be worth over $5,000 at current prices.

gloss.en: the subjects taught in a school or course

words.example: The school curriculum should be as broad as possible.

gloss.en: a moving mark showing your place on a screen

words.example: Move the cursor to the top of the page.

gloss.en: a line that bends smoothly

words.example: The road follows the curve of the river.

gloss.en: having a round, bending shape

words.example: The table has a smooth curved edge.

gloss.en: pretty and attractive

words.example: They have a cute little baby.

gloss.en: someone who cannot stop using the internet

words.example: He became a cyberaddict who never went outside.

gloss.en: a cafe where you can use the internet

words.example: We checked our email at a cybercafe.

gloss.en: crime committed using computers or the internet

words.example: The police have a team that fights cybercrime.

gloss.en: a digital pet kept on a computer or device

words.example: As a kid she loved feeding her cyberpet.

gloss.en: a school where lessons happen online

words.example: He studies at home through a cyberschool.

gloss.en: the imagined world of computers and the internet

words.example: Their friendship began in cyberspace.

gloss.en: a person who rides a bicycle

words.example: A cyclist rode past us on the path.

gloss.en: silly or foolish

words.example: That was a daft thing to say.

gloss.en: made from milk

words.example: She avoids dairy products like cheese.

gloss.en: used to show anger or annoyance

words.example: I can't find my damn keys again.

gloss.en: a wild plant with bright yellow flowers

words.example: A yellow dandelion grew in the grass.

gloss.en: to be brave enough to do something

words.example: She said it as loudly as she dared.

gloss.en: the state of being dark, with no light

words.example: After a few minutes our eyes got used to the darkness.

gloss.en: much loved; very dear

words.example: Come here, my darling child.

gloss.en: a small amount; a quick run

words.example: Add a dash of salt to the soup.

gloss.en: facts or information used to make decisions

words.example: We collected data over a 10-day period.

gloss.en: an organized set of data stored in a computer

words.example: The database is updated monthly.

gloss.en: a particular day of the month or year

words.example: What's the date today?

gloss.en: old-fashioned; no longer modern

words.example: His clothes look very dated now.

gloss.en: the wife of your son

words.example: His daughter-in-law cooked dinner for everyone.

gloss.en: the time of day when it is light

words.example: The shop is only open in the daytime.

gloss.en: a time by which something must be done

words.example: I prefer to work to a deadline.

gloss.en: likely to cause death

words.example: The snake's bite can be deadly.

gloss.en: a person who buys and sells a product

words.example: He bought the painting from an art dealer.

gloss.en: very much; with deep feeling

words.example: She loved her family dearly.

gloss.en: a formal discussion of opposing views

words.example: They watched the first televised presidential debate.

gloss.en: an amount of money taken from an account

words.example: The bank showed a debit of fifty dollars.

gloss.en: a sum of money that someone owes

words.example: It took him years to pay the debt.

gloss.en: a period of ten years

words.example: They lived abroad for a decade.

gloss.en: to be slowly destroyed by natural processes

words.example: Sugar makes your teeth decay.

gloss.en: to make someone believe something untrue

words.example: He tried to deceive his parents about the money.

gloss.en: of a good enough standard or quality

words.example: They finally found a decent place to live.

gloss.en: likely to make you believe something untrue

words.example: The calm sea was deceptive and dangerous.

gloss.en: the top outside floor of a ship

words.example: I was the only person on deck that night.

gloss.en: to say something officially or publicly

words.example: The government has declared a state of emergency.

gloss.en: a continuous decrease in number or quality

words.example: There has been a decline in student numbers.

gloss.en: the process of decaying or breaking down

words.example: The decomposition of leaves enriches the soil.

gloss.en: to make something look more attractive

words.example: They decorated the room with flowers and balloons.

gloss.en: a thing that makes something look attractive

words.example: We put up Christmas decorations in the hall.

gloss.en: intended to look attractive rather than useful

words.example: The plates are purely decorative.

gloss.en: the process or amount of reducing something

words.example: Sales show a decrease compared with last year.

gloss.en: to work something out from facts you know

words.example: From the clues she could deduce his name.

gloss.en: a conclusion reached by reasoning; an amount taken away

words.example: His clever deduction solved the case.

gloss.en: going far down from the top or surface

words.example: They dug a deep hole in the garden.

gloss.en: very much; to a great degree

words.example: She is deeply religious.

gloss.en: a wild animal that can run fast and has horns

words.example: A deer ran across the field.

gloss.en: failure to win or succeed

words.example: The party faces defeat in the election.

gloss.en: the act of protecting from attack or criticism

words.example: They are soldiers who died in defence of their country.

gloss.en: to protect someone or something from attack

words.example: The role of the military is to defend the country.

gloss.en: a player who stops the other team scoring

words.example: He is a strong defender on the football team.

gloss.en: the cutting down of forests over a large area

words.example: Deforestation harms many wild animals.

gloss.en: to take away people's human qualities

words.example: War can dehumanise both sides.

gloss.en: to take away people's human qualities

words.example: War can dehumanize both sides.

gloss.en: a time of waiting because something is late

words.example: Commuters will face long delays on the roads today.

gloss.en: to remove something written or stored

words.example: Your name has been deleted from the list.

gloss.en: done on purpose, not by accident

words.example: The fire was a deliberate act of vandalism.

gloss.en: in a planned way, on purpose

words.example: She's been deliberately ignoring him all day.

gloss.en: a special, rare food; gentle care

words.example: This cheese is a local delicacy.

gloss.en: in a careful, gentle way

words.example: She delicately picked up the tiny flower.

gloss.en: a feeling of great pleasure

words.example: The children screamed with delight.

gloss.en: very pleased

words.example: She gave a delighted smile.

gloss.en: the act of taking goods to people

words.example: Allow 28 days for delivery.

gloss.en: a strong request; a need for something

words.example: The workers made a demand for higher pay.

gloss.en: needing a lot of effort, skill, or attention

words.example: Caring for a baby is a demanding job.

gloss.en: a system where people vote for their leaders

words.example: They are proud to live in a democracy.

gloss.en: based on elected representatives of the people

words.example: They live in a democratic country.

gloss.en: to pull down or destroy a building

words.example: They plan to demolish the old factory.

gloss.en: to show something clearly with proof

words.example: The results demonstrate that the plan works.

gloss.en: a public protest; a show of how to do something

words.example: Thousands took part in the demonstration.

gloss.en: a strong cotton cloth used for jeans

words.example: He wore a jacket made of denim.

gloss.en: in a thick or crowded way

words.example: This is a densely populated city.

gloss.en: relating to the teeth

words.example: She needs to book a dental appointment.

gloss.en: to say that something is not true

words.example: He continued to deny the charge.

gloss.en: to leave a place, especially on a trip

words.example: Flights for Rome depart from Terminal 3.

gloss.en: able to be trusted or relied on

words.example: He is a dependable friend who always helps.

gloss.en: needing someone or something to survive

words.example: She is a woman with several dependent children.

gloss.en: very sad and without hope

words.example: You mustn't let yourself get depressed.

gloss.en: making you feel very sad

words.example: The grey weather was depressing.

gloss.en: a condition of deep sadness and hopelessness

words.example: She was diagnosed as having clinical depression.

gloss.en: the distance from the top to the bottom

words.example: What's the depth of the water here?

gloss.en: to get something from a source

words.example: She derives great pleasure from reading.

gloss.en: describing something in detail

words.example: The book has a rich, descriptive style.

gloss.en: a large dry area with little water or plants

words.example: They crossed the Sahara Desert by camel.

gloss.en: to earn a reward or punishment by your actions

words.example: You deserve a rest after all that hard work.

gloss.en: made by a famous, fashionable designer

words.example: She only wears designer clothes.

gloss.en: a strong wish to have or do something

words.example: He now had enough money to satisfy his desires.

gloss.en: emptiness and ruin; deep loneliness

gloss.en: having little hope and ready to do anything
